title: USD Schema Registry Capability
purpose: Describe the implementation-defined runtime input that supplies typed and applied schema definitions used to build prim definitions, schema-defined properties, and schema fallback values.

included: runtime registration of typed schema definitions
runtime registration of applied single and multiple schema definitions
schema property definitions with kind, metadata fields, and fallback default values
applied schema instance-name substitution for multiple applied schemas
fallbackPrimTypes substitution for unregistered typed schema names when a registered fallback type is available
override properties for sparse strengthening of included schema property fields
schema inclusion and override-property contract diagnostics
deferred: file format or plugin mechanism used to discover schema definitions
schema family version selection beyond exact registered schema names
domain behavior for concrete schemas such as collections, color, xforms, materials, or geometry

model.SchemaRegistry: Target-native registry of schema definitions. The AOUSD v1.0.1 schemas chapter leaves ingestion and runtime registration mechanisms implementation-defined.
model.SchemaDefinition: Typed or applied schema declaration containing properties and optional inheritance metadata.
model.SchemaPropertyDefinition: Schema-defined property declaration containing property kind and typed metadata/fallback fields.
model.PrimDefinition: The composed schema context for one composed prim, including schema order and schema-defined property definitions.

laws: schema definition ingestion is an implementation boundary and must not be inferred from OpenUSD registry behavior

registered schema names, applied schema names, instance names, property names, field names, and type names must use semantic token/name handles rather than parser-local strings in generated domain logic
schema-defined properties are weaker than authored composed property specs on the prim
schema built-ins and auto-applies are explicit registry data and must not be inferred from OpenUSD
override properties may sparsely strengthen included schema properties but must not define new properties or override typeName and variability
schema fallback defaults must be reported as schema-defined fallback data, not as authored layer opinions
fallbackPrimTypes is interpreted on the composed prim, not as layer-level schema semantics
